# Libraries
import os
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy.stats import normaltest, probplot
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error

# Loading the dataset
df = pd.read_csv('used_cars_D214.csv')

OUTPUT_DIR = Path("output")
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# Check for missing values
missing_values = df.isnull().sum()
missing_percent = (df.isnull().sum() / len(df)) * 100

missing_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Missing Values": missing_values,
    "Percent Missing": missing_percent.round(2)
}).sort_values(by="Missing Values", ascending=False)

print("\nMissing Value Summary:")
print(missing_summary)

missing_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "missing_values_summary.csv")

In [10]:
# Clean Price column
df["price"] = df["price"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[\$,]", "", regex=True)
df["price"] = pd.to_numeric(df["price"], errors="coerce")
In [11]:
# Clean mileage column
df["mileage"] = df["mileage"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.]", "", regex=True)
df["mileage"] = pd.to_numeric(df["mileage"], errors="coerce")
In [12]:
# Features
current_year = 2026

# Vehicle age
df["vehicle_age"] = current_year - df["model_year"]

# Extract horsepower from engine text if available
df["horsepower"] = df["engine"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)HP", expand=False)
df["horsepower"] = pd.to_numeric(df["horsepower"], errors="coerce")

# Extract engine liters from engine text if available
df["engine_liters"] = df["engine"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)L", expand=False)
df["engine_liters"] = pd.to_numeric(df["engine_liters"], errors="coerce")

# Simplify transmission values
def simplify_transmission(x):
    x = str(x).strip().lower()
    if "manual" in x or "m/t" in x:
        return "Manual"
    elif "cvt" in x:
        return "CVT"
    elif "automatic" in x or "a/t" in x:
        return "Automatic"
    else:
        return "Other"

df["transmission_group"] = df["transmission"].apply(simplify_transmission)

# Fill missing categories
df["fuel_type"] = df["fuel_type"].fillna("Unknown")
df["accident"] = df["accident"].fillna("Unknown")
df["clean_title"] = df["clean_title"].fillna("Unknown")
df["brand"] = df["brand"].fillna("Unknown")

# Accident flag
df["accident_flag"] = df["accident"].map({
    "None reported": 0,
    "At least 1 accident or damage reported": 1,
    "Unknown": np.nan
})

# Clean title flag
df["clean_title_flag"] = df["clean_title"].map({
    "Yes": 1,
    "Unknown": np.nan
})

# Keep top 15 brands, all others become Other
top_brands = df["brand"].value_counts().head(15).index
df["brand_group"] = df["brand"].apply(lambda x: x if x in top_brands else "Other")

print("\nShape after feature engineering:", df.shape)

Shape after feature engineering: (4009, 19)
In [13]:
# Remove invalid rows
df = df[df["price"] > 0]
df = df[df["mileage"] >= 0]
df = df[df["vehicle_age"] >= 0]

print("\nShape after removing invalid rows:", df.shape)

Shape after removing invalid rows: (4009, 19)
In [14]:
# Check for outliers using IQR
numeric_cols = ["price", "mileage", "horsepower", "engine_liters"]

print("\nOutlier counts before removal:")
outlier_summary_list = []

for col in numeric_cols:
    q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
    upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr

    outlier_count = df[(df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper)].shape[0]

    print(f"{col}: {outlier_count} outliers")

    outlier_summary_list.append({
        "Column": col,
        "Q1": q1,
        "Q3": q3,
        "IQR": iqr,
        "Lower Bound": lower,
        "Upper Bound": upper,
        "Outlier Count": outlier_count
    })

outlier_summary = pd.DataFrame(outlier_summary_list)
outlier_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "outlier_summary_before_removal.csv", index=False)

Outlier counts before removal:
price: 244 outliers
mileage: 69 outliers
horsepower: 63 outliers
engine_liters: 6 outliers
In [15]:
# Remove outliers one column at a time using IQR
for col in numeric_cols:
    q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
    upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr

    df = df[(df[col].isna()) | ((df[col] >= lower) & (df[col] <= upper))]

print("\nShape after outlier removal:", df.shape)

Shape after outlier removal: (3648, 19)
In [16]:
# Normality test
# Original price
price_stat, price_p = normaltest(df["price"].dropna())
print(price_p)

4.1635082309595784e-76
In [17]:
# Log-transform price
df["log_price"] = np.log1p(df["price"])
log_price_stat, log_price_p = normaltest(df["log_price"].dropna())

normality_results = pd.DataFrame({
    "Variable": ["price", "log_price"],
    "Statistic": [price_stat, log_price_stat],
    "p_value": [price_p, log_price_p]
})

print("\nNormality Test Results:")
print(normality_results)

normality_results.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "normality_results.csv", index=False)

Normality Test Results:
    Variable   Statistic       p_value
0      price  347.140218  4.163508e-76
1  log_price  167.399177  4.464065e-37

In [25]:
# Selecting features and target
features = [
    "mileage",
    "vehicle_age",
    "horsepower",
    "engine_liters",
    "fuel_type",
    "transmission_group",
    "brand_group",
    "accident_flag",
    "clean_title_flag"
]

target = "log_price"

X = df[features]
y = df[target]
In [26]:
# Train/test split
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.20, random_state=42
)

print("\nTraining set shape:", X_train.shape)
print("Testing set shape:", X_test.shape)

Training set shape: (2918, 9)
Testing set shape: (730, 9)
In [27]:
# Preprocesssing pipeline
numeric_features = [
    "mileage",
    "vehicle_age",
    "horsepower",
    "engine_liters",
    "accident_flag",
    "clean_title_flag"
]

categorical_features = [
    "fuel_type",
    "transmission_group",
    "brand_group"
]

numeric_transformer = Pipeline(steps=[
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median"))
])

categorical_transformer = Pipeline(steps=[
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
    ("onehot", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore"))
])

preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", numeric_transformer, numeric_features),
        ("cat", categorical_transformer, categorical_features)
    ]
)
In [28]:
# Multiple linear regression model
model = Pipeline(steps=[
    ("preprocessor", preprocessor),
    ("regressor", LinearRegression())
])

model.fit(X_train, y_train)
Out[28]:
Pipeline(steps=[('preprocessor',
                 ColumnTransformer(transformers=[('num',
                                                  Pipeline(steps=[('imputer',
                                                                   SimpleImputer(strategy='median'))]),
                                                  ['mileage', 'vehicle_age',
                                                   'horsepower',
                                                   'engine_liters',
                                                   'accident_flag',
                                                   'clean_title_flag']),
                                                 ('cat',
                                                  Pipeline(steps=[('imputer',
                                                                   SimpleImputer(strategy='most_frequent')),
                                                                  ('onehot',
                                                                   OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown='ignore'))]),
                                                  ['fuel_type',
                                                   'transmission_group',
                                                   'brand_group'])])),
                ('regressor', LinearRegression())])

In [29]:
# Predictions
y_pred_log = model.predict(X_test)

# Convert back to original dollar scale
y_test_price = np.expm1(y_test)
y_pred_price = np.expm1(y_pred_log)
In [30]:
# Model evaluation
r2_log = r2_score(y_test, y_pred_log)
r2_price = r2_score(y_test_price, y_pred_price)
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test_price, y_pred_price))
mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test_price, y_pred_price)

print("\nModel Performance:")
print("R² on log(price):", round(r2_log, 4))
print("R² on original price scale:", round(r2_price, 4))
print("RMSE:", round(rmse, 2))
print("MAE:", round(mae, 2))

if r2_log >= 0.70:
    print("The model meets the 70% accuracy threshold based on R².")
else:
    print("The model does NOT meet the 70% accuracy threshold based on R².")

Model Performance:
R² on log(price): 0.7537
R² on original price scale: 0.6476
RMSE: 12567.41
MAE: 8282.31
The model meets the 70% accuracy threshold based on R².
In [31]:
# Residual check
residuals = y_test - y_pred_log
residual_stat, residual_p = normaltest(residuals)

print("\nResidual normality test p-value:", residual_p)

residual_normality_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Statistic": [residual_stat],
    "p_value": [residual_p]
})
residual_normality_df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "residual_normality.csv", index=False)

Residual normality test p-value: 2.6356397943137252e-14
In [32]:
# Q-Q plot of residuals
plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
probplot(residuals, dist="norm", plot=plt)
plt.title("Q-Q Plot of Residuals")
plt.show()
In [33]:
# Actual vs predicted plot
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.scatter(y_test_price, y_pred_price, alpha=0.5)
plt.title("Actual vs Predicted Used Car Prices")
plt.xlabel("Actual Price")
plt.ylabel("Predicted Price")
plt.show()
In [34]:
# Regression coefficients
feature_names = model.named_steps["preprocessor"].get_feature_names_out()
coefficients = model.named_steps["regressor"].coef_

coef_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Feature": feature_names,
    "Coefficient": coefficients
})

coef_df["Absolute_Coefficient"] = coef_df["Coefficient"].abs()
coef_df = coef_df.sort_values(by="Absolute_Coefficient", ascending=False)

print("\nTop 15 coefficients:")
print(coef_df.head(15))

coef_df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "regression_coefficients.csv", index=False)

In [35]:
# Save cleaned data and model summary
df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "cleaned_used_cars.csv", index=False)

prediction_results = pd.DataFrame({
    "Actual Price": y_test_price.values,
    "Predicted Price": y_pred_price,
    "Residual (Log Scale)": residuals.values
})
prediction_results.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "prediction_results.csv", index=False)

model_summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": ["R2_log_price", "R2_price_scale", "RMSE", "MAE", "Residual_Normality_p"],
    "Value": [r2_log, r2_price, rmse, mae, residual_p]
})
model_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "model_summary.csv", index=False)

print("\nAll outputs saved to:")
print(OUTPUT_DIR)
